author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
Faith the Ground of All Righteousness.
title: Faith the Ground of All Righteousness.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: The righteousness of the law is proposed in these terms,—that whosoever shall do it shall live in it; and the purpose is, that when each has discovered his own weakness, he may not by his own strength, nor by the letter of the law (which cannot be done), but by faith, conciliating the Justifier, attain, and do, and live in it.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: For the work in which he who does it shall live, is not done except by one who is justified.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: His justification, however, is obtained by faith; and concerning faith it is written, “Say not in thine heart, Who shall ascend into heaven? (that is, to bring down Christ therefrom;) or, Who shall descend into the deep? (that is, to bring up Christ again from the dead.)

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: The word is nigh thee, even in thy mouth, and in thy heart: that is (says he), the word of faith which we preach: That if thou shalt confess with thy mouth the Lord Jesus, and shalt believe in thine heart that God hath raised Him from the dead, thou shalt be saved.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: As far as he is saved, so far is he righteous.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: For by this faith we believe that God will raise even us from the dead,—even now in the spirit, that we may in this present world live soberly, righteously, and godly in the renewal of His grace; and by and by in our flesh, which shall rise again to immortality, which indeed is the reward of the Spirit, who precedes it by a resurrection which is appropriate to Himself,—that is, by justification.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: “For we are buried with Christ by baptism unto death, that like as Christ was raised up from the dead by the glory of the Father, even so we also should walk in newness of life.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: By faith, therefore, in Jesus Christ we obtain salvation,—both in so far as it is begun within us in reality, and in so far as its perfection is waited for in hope; “for whosoever shall call on the name of the Lord shall be saved.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: “How abundant,” says the Psalmist, “is the multitude of Thy goodness, O Lord, which Thou hast laid up for them that fear Thee, and hast perfected for them that hope in Thee!”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: By the law we fear God; by faith we hope in God: but from those who fear punishment grace is hidden.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: And the soul which labours under this fear, since it has not conquered its evil concupiscence, and from which this fear, like a harsh master, has not departed,—let it flee by faith for refuge to the mercy of God, that He may give it what He commands, and may, by inspiring into it the sweetness of His grace through His Holy Spirit, cause the soul to delight more in what He teaches it, than it delights in what opposes His instruction.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: In this manner it is that the great abundance of His sweetness,—that is, the law of faith,—His love which is in our hearts, and shed abroad, is perfected in them that hope in Him, that good may be wrought by the soul, healed not by the fear of punishment, but by the love of righteousness.
